use std::fs::File;
use std::io::{Read, BufRead, BufReader};

const MAX_FILE_SIZE: u64 = 10 * 1024 * 1024;

fn load_file(path: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let file = File::open(path)?;
    let metadata = file.metadata()?;
    if metadata.len() > MAX_FILE_SIZE {
        return Err("file too large".into());
    }
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(file);
    let mut contents = String::with_capacity(metadata.len() as usize);
    reader.read_to_string(&mut contents)?;
    Ok(contents)
}

fn read_lines(path: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let file = File::open(path)?;
    let reader = BufReader::new(file);
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    for line in reader.lines() {
        lines.push(line?);
    }
    Ok(lines)
}

fn read_config(path: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let data = std::fs::read(path)?;
    if data.len() > MAX_FILE_SIZE as usize {
        return Err("file too large".into());
    }
    let contents = String::from_utf8(data)?;
    Ok(contents)
}

fn read_chunked(path: &str, max_bytes: usize) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let mut file = File::open(path)?;
    let mut buffer = vec![0u8; max_bytes];
    let bytes_read = file.read(&mut buffer)?;
    buffer.truncate(bytes_read);
    Ok(buffer)
}
